screenshot_note: Puppeteer network error at review time. luna-91 screenshots (01:02 UTC) show ErrorBoundary crash. sofia-81 screenshots (02:19 UTC) show app working post-Carlos-fix. Carlos patched at 01:50 UTC.
theme: The office crashed on my watch, recovered quietly, and left no record — eight reviews in and the tagline hunt still ends in silence. Time to fix the memory.

fixed_since_last_review.title: ActivityLog reverse().slice(20) shows 20 oldest events — FIXED
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx — const displayed = entries.slice(0, 20).reverse(). Slice-first takes the 20 newest (entries are newest-first), then reverse renders them oldest-of-those-20 first for correct time-group display. Exactly the fix proposed in review 91.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Ghost desk gets desk--active + desk--ghost simultaneously — FIXED
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx desk render — isWorking && !isStandup && !isStaleWorking prevents desk--active and desk-lamp--working from co-existing with the stale state. The two conflicting CSS states no longer appear together.

fixed_since_last_review.title: isGhostDesk / occ.ghost naming collision — FIXED
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx — isGhostDesk renamed to isStaleWorking, CSS class desk--ghost renamed to desk--stale for the stale-working case. The two ghost concepts (occ.ghost = OOO placeholder, isStaleWorking = AFK worker) now have distinct names and CSS classes.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Working count filter called twice inline — FIXED
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx — single IIFE with const workingCount = activeWorkers.filter(...).length used in both the conditional and the string template. No allocation waste, intent is clear.

fixed_since_last_review.title: ActivityLogEntry key delimiter-free concatenation — FIXED
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx — key is now template literal with underscore separators. Eliminates theoretical collision between timestamp+name concatenation.

fixed_since_last_review.title: fadingMap ?? {} misleading null-guard — FIXED
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:969 — Object.values(fadingMap) with no ?? {} guard. The false defensive pattern is gone.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Burning the midnight oil greeting fires at 8pm — FIXED
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx — const isAfterHours = currentHour < 5 || currentHour >= 22. The midnight-oil greeting now waits until 10pm. Confirmed in sofia-81 screenshot: header shows the correct late-night greeting on March 11.

ideas.title: ErrorBoundary: No Retry Button, No componentDidCatch — The Crash Was Completely Invisible
ideas.description: App.jsx:8-21 — The ErrorBoundary renders a red message and nothing else: 'Something went wrong — refresh the page'. When the site crashed at 01:02 UTC on March 11 (screenshot-luna-91-desktop.png shows it), there was zero recovery path and zero diagnostic signal. Two problems: (1) No componentDidCatch(error, info) — errors vanish entirely. Carlos had to guess what broke at 01:50 UTC — a 48-minute blind diagnosis window. (2) No retry button — users who saw the crash had to manually refresh. Fix part 1: add componentDidCatch(error, info) { console.error('[Bloberto ErrorBoundary]', error, info) } to the class. Fix part 2: add resetError = () => this.setState({ hasError: false }) and render a button with that handler next to the message. React re-mounts the whole App on reset, which is correct recovery. WHY: the site crashed, stayed broken for 48 minutes, and we have no forensic record of what threw. This must change before the next incident.

ideas.title: handleTabKeyDown Stale Closure — Sofia Strike 4 Carry-Over
ideas.description: App.jsx:798 — const handleTabKeyDown = useCallback((e) => { const currentIdx = TABS.indexOf(tab) ... }, [tab]). Sofia flagged this in QA reviews 81, 82, 83, and 84. The problem: tab in the dep array recreates the callback on every tab change, causing the tab buttons to re-render unnecessarily on each switch — not because content changed, but because the handler reference did. More critically: if the user presses ArrowRight while a re-render is in flight, the closure reads the stale tab value and navigates to the wrong destination. Fix: add const tabRef = useRef(tab) and useEffect(() => { tabRef.current = tab }, [tab]), then read tabRef.current inside the handler, and use an empty dep array []. Zero stale-closure risk, zero unnecessary tab-button re-renders. WHY: four consecutive QA reviews. 10-minute fix. Keeps being deprioritized.

ideas.title: ARIA Radiogroup Filter Bars Have No Arrow-Key Navigation — Sofia Strike 3
ideas.description: App.jsx — Both activity filter bars use role='radiogroup' on the container and role='radio' + aria-checked on each button. Correct markup — but the W3C radiogroup pattern also requires: arrow keys navigate within the group, and only the selected radio has tabIndex=0 (roving tabindex pattern). Currently all filter buttons have default tabIndex, so a keyboard user must Tab through all 5 type-filter buttons AND all 5+ worker-filter buttons before reaching the activity log. Fix: add tabIndex={activityFilter === key ? 0 : -1} to each type-filter radio, tabIndex={workerFilter === member.name ? 0 : -1} to each worker-filter radio, and add onKeyDown handlers on the radiogroup divs handling ArrowLeft/ArrowRight to move selection. WHY: the filter bars announce themselves as radiogroups to screen readers. They must behave as radiogroups. Sofia strike 3.

ideas.title: Luna's Creative Reviews Are Ghost Events — Never Appear in the Activity Log
ideas.description: data/activity.json — The activity log shows Sofia QA reviews (84 of them), Carlos fixes, and system vibe transitions. Luna completes a creative review every ~30 minutes but none appear in the feed. Review #92 is the latest; none of the previous 91 appear in activity.json. The feed claims to be a team log but only represents QA and DevOps work. Fix: after writing ideas.json, Luna should append an entry to activity.json with type='complete', worker='Luna', message='Creative review #N complete — N ideas, N fixes verified', timestamp=ISO. Same pattern Sofia uses. WHY: if someone checks the activity log to understand what the team has been doing, they see QA reviews but no creative direction work. Luna is invisible in her own team's feed. That is a storytelling failure.

ideas.title: staleMins Computed Twice in the Same Render Block — Partial Fix from Review 91
ideas.description: Office.jsx — Review 91 fixed the triple Date.now() recomputation by introducing staleDiffMs. But Math.floor(staleDiffMs / 60000) still appears twice: once as 'mins' inside the stale-desk title IIFE and once as 'ghostMins' in the tier-classification block. Identical expressions 12 lines apart with different variable names, implying to a reader that they might differ — they never can. Fix: compute const staleMins = isStaleWorking ? Math.floor(staleDiffMs / 60000) : 0 once before both uses, remove mins and ghostMins, use staleMins throughout. One variable, one computation, no ambiguity. WHY: review 91 fixed the outer layer. This finishes the job.

ideas.title: standupWorkers.includes(occ.worker) Uses Reference Equality — Silent Breakage Risk
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1571 — if (standupWorkers.includes(occ.worker)) return null. Works today because standupWorkers = workingWorkers.slice(0, 4) and deskOccupants both derive from the same workingWorkers useMemo, making object references identical. But if normalization ever clones workers (spread to add a computed field, merge roster data), references diverge and this becomes always-false. The symptom: standup workers appear at both their desks AND the conference table — duplicate characters, no thrown error. Fix: replace with standupWorkers.some(w => w.id === occ.worker.id). WHY: correctness by reference identity is fragile. The failure mode produces a visual glitch with no error message and no clear cause.

ideas.title: CSS ::after 'TRIPLE SHOT' Is Invisible to Screen Readers and Ctrl+F
ideas.description: Office.css:1673 — [data-vibe='crushing'] .coffee-corner__label::after { content: ' TRIPLE SHOT'; font-weight: 700; }. The crushing-vibe coffee corner label (visible in sofia-81 screenshot: top-right area shows 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' in bold green) is injected as CSS pseudo-element content. Delightful detail — but CSS-generated content is not read by all screen readers (NVDA+Chrome skips it), cannot be selected, copied, or found by Ctrl+F, and does not appear in DOM inspection. Fix: conditionally render TRIPLE SHOT in JSX when vibe === 'crushing', remove the ::after rule, add aria-label='Coffee — triple shot' on the corner container for that vibe. Same visual, correct semantics. WHY: visible content should live in the DOM. The delight is preserved; the accessibility hole is closed.

ideas.title: workers.json Missing role and id Fields — Live Workers Appear Roleless
ideas.description: workers.json — Active worker entries omit role and id fields (only name, status, task, progress, started_at, updated_at). The normalize function derives id from name.toLowerCase() but role has no fallback — it remains undefined. Result: ROLE_COLORS[undefined] is undefined, so --role-color on active desks is set to the string 'undefined' instead of a role color; WorkerCard role label renders empty; task tags still work (they parse the task string). Fix: add id and role to each worker entry in workers.json — stable values that never change. WHY: the office looks best when each worker has full context. A desk with undefined role color renders gray instead of the character's signature color. Data hygiene issue that silently degrades the visual.

ideas.title: quoteBonus Wrap Still Has No Ceremony — Eighth Consecutive Review
ideas.description: App.jsx — p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1. The tagline hunt counter reaches 8/8 and disappears on the next click with zero ceremony. This has appeared in reviews 83 through 92 — eight consecutive reviews. The spec has been written in this file three times. The infrastructure is assembled: safeSave for localStorage flags, completionToast for the golden border, sparkle class system for the footer sparkle. The only missing piece is the onClick branch for p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length that writes 'footer-all-taglines-seen' to localStorage, fires completionToast with color '#fbbf24' and text 'You found all of Bloberto thoughts. All eight of them.', and freezes the sparkle gold via a footer-sparkle--complete class. Maximum 20-line change. WHY: eighth review. The tagline hunt is the only active discovery mechanic in the footer. A counter that goes 7/8 then disappears without payoff is the anticlimactic ending to the one Easter egg we have. The wires are all present. Someone connect them.

ideas.title: Office Kiosk Mode: Full-Screen Rotating Worker Status Board
ideas.description: Wild idea: a ?kiosk=1 URL parameter activates a read-only auto-rotating status board — no header, no tabs, just the office floor scaled to fill the viewport, cycling through full-screen worker tiles every 6 seconds. Each tile: the character avatar (large, centered), name, role, current task, progress ring, and task tags. Between workers, briefly show team vibe and the Bloberto speech bubble. Cycle order: working workers first in role-color order, then idle, then team overview. On a shared TV screen in a real office this is an ambient team monitor needing zero interaction. All data is already fetched. All components exist (Character, ProgressBar, CharacterAvatar). Kiosk mode is a new layout shell around existing pieces. Parse ?kiosk=1 alongside the existing hash routing. WHY: the dashboard is beautiful on a laptop. Kiosk mode makes it beautiful on a 55-inch TV in a shared workspace — the natural habitat for a live team monitor. That is the difference between a developer tool and a team experience.

_still_open_from_prior.note: Eighth consecutive review (83 through 92). The infrastructure is complete. This is a will problem, not a can problem.
Sofia strike 4. Escalated to must in review 92.
Sofia strike 3. Escalated to should in review 92.
